content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 13, 1990


Honorable Robert A. Hillery
P.O. Box 5001
68-625 Perez Road
Cathedral City, CA  92235-5001

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-419

Dear Mayor Hillery:

You are seeking advice regarding your duties and
responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your City Attorney, Allen
Briggs, indicated by phone that you were not seeking a formal
opinion, but rather written advice pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)
(copy enclosed). This letter is based on the facts presented to
us.²

QUESTION

The agent who represented the other party in a real estate
transaction with you now appears before the City Council as a
principal on matters unrelated to the prior transaction. May you
participate in the decision now before the City Council?

CONCLUSION

You have no conflict of interest under the Act because you
appear to have no financial interest in the decision before the
Council.


_______________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 The Commission does not act as a finder of fact. The advice
provided in this letter is applicable only to the extent the facts
provided are correct and that all material facts have been
presented. (In re Ogelsby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71, copy enclosed).


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

File No. A-90-419
Page 2

FACTS

Pursuant to an agreement entered into on August 21, 1989,
Federated Development Company will trade a tract of land for your
land if public ingress to your property becomes impossible.
Stanley Kibrick acted as agent for Federated Development Company
and conducted most of the negotiations, although he did not sign
the agreement.

Mr. Kibrick is now appearing before the Cathedral City
Council and the Redevelopment Agency with a proposal for a new
development.  He is seeking action on land more than a mile from
any property owned by you, and you state that his proposal will
have no material financial effect on you or any property owned by
you. Mr. Kibrick is acting on behalf of his own separate development company and Federated Development Company is not involved.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits any public official from participating in a
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. (Section 87100.)

An official has a disqualifying financial interest in a decision if the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable material
financial effect on, among other things, (1) the official, (2) a
source of income who paid the official $250 or more during the 12
months preceding the decision, (3) the official's real property
worth $1,000 or more. (Section 87103(a), (b), and (c); Regulation
18702, copy enclosed.)

The decision on Mr. Kibrick's proposal will have no financial
effect on you or your real property. Moreover, Mr. Kibrick is not
a source of income to you. (Section 82030.) Because you have no
financial interest as described in Section 87103, you have no
conflict of interest under the Act.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Peggy Bernardy
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:PB:plh

Enclosures
